episodes.title: Lucy and the Military
episodes.desc: Lucy tries to sneak into a Military Academy disguised as a cadet to visit her son, Jerry, before visitor's day.

episodes.title: Lucy's College Reunion
episodes.desc: Lucy goes to her college reunion. While she's there, she helps Chris and some of her friends steal the bell tower statue. She and Viv end up having to put it back or suffer the consiquences. They get locked on the top of the tower, until the dean discovers them.

episodes.title: The Loophole in the Lease
episodes.desc: Sherman leaves the water in the bathtub running and it runs over, making the plaster in the ceiling below collapse. Mr. Mooney convinces Lucy that it's Viv's fault and they threaten to throw Viv out if she doesn't pay for the damages. They take out their lease and in it, it says that the rent will be considered down payment for the house, after five years, if the tenant desires. Viv has Mr. Mooney and Lucy thinking that she will and they end up trying to steal it from her, while she's sleeping.

episodes.title: Lucy Conducts the Symphony
episodes.desc: Viv's cousin comes to New York to play percussion in Manhattan. When he arrives at the house, he's a nervous wreck and Lucy tries to relax him by telling jokes, but gets the hicups from laughing. Lucy tries to cure him by hypnotizing him, but she puts him in a heavy sleep and he won't wake up. Lucy ends up playing in the symphony in his place and makes the conductor mad and she takes his place.

episodes.title: Lucy Plays Florence Nightingale
episodes.desc: Lucy needs Mr. Mooney to give her some money to buy Chris a dress for a dance, but Mr. Mooney is in the hospital with a broken leg. Lucy is a hospital helper and on the next day she has work, she tries to get Mr. Mooney to sign the check. She gets kicked off the floor Mr. Mooney is staying on and dresses as a doctor to get into his room.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes to Art Class
episodes.desc: When both Lucy and Viv enroll in an art class to win the affections of handsome John Brooks III, Vivian tricks Lucy into posing for the class so she can get a date with Brooks.

episodes.title: Chris Goes Steady
episodes.desc: Chris decides to go steady with a boy who turns out to be Mr. Mooney's son.  Lucy and Mr. Mooney don't like it and team up to show the kids a lesson by teaching them the real responsiblities of a serious relationship.  And then the two adults must camp out in a treehouse to make sure the two don't elope.

episodes.title: Lucy Takes Up Golf
episodes.desc: Lucy takes up golf to keep a man interested in her.

episodes.title: Lucy Teaches Ethel Merman to Sing
episodes.desc: When Lucy happens upon Ethel Merman at the bank, then Ethel and Mr. Mooney must make up a fake name and tell Lucy that she only looks like Ethel Merman.  Soon after, Lucy is in need of money, as usual, and decides to let this Ethel Merman look alike rent a room.  Soon after Lucy is giving her lessons how to perform and sing like Ethel Merman.

episodes.title: Ethel Merman and the Boy Scout Show
episodes.desc: When Lucy discovers that Ethel Merman and Mr. Mooney made up a story to hid the singer's real identity, she can't help but come up with the nerve to as Ethel to perform at her son's boy scout pageant.  Ethel accepts and she does the show, along with Lucy and Mr. Mooney.

episodes.title: Lucy and Viv Open a Restaurant
episodes.desc: Lucy and Viv buy a restaurant and every theme they make it, flops.

episodes.title: Lucy Takes a Job at the Bank
episodes.desc: Mr. Mooney hires Lucy to give a new toaster to anyone who opens a savings account.  Lucy tries to drum up business by getting her friends to close out their savings accounts and then redeposit the money as new accounts, leading to a rumor that the bank is failing!

episodes.title: Viv Moves Out
episodes.desc: Lucy and Viv have a fight and Viv decides to move out. Mr. Mooney makes Lucy get a new tenant and Viv wants to move back in.

episodes.title: Lucy Is Her Own Lawyer
episodes.desc: A dog in the neighborhood barks all night and Lucy decides to sue the owner, Mr. Mooney!

episodes.title: Lucy Meets a Millionaire
episodes.desc: When Italian millionaire Umberto Fabriani asks Lucy on a date, Lucy spills spaghetti sauce all over Mr. Fabriani's best suit, compelling her and Mr. Mooney to break into the cleaners.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes into Politics
episodes.desc: Lucy and Viv want to get into Mr. Mooney's political campaign. Mr. Mooney tells Lucy to make an important call, but Lucy forgets.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Scout Trip
episodes.desc: Lucy and Viv have to replace a man on the scout trip and Lucy gets lost in the woods.

episodes.title: Lucy Is a Process Server
episodes.desc: Lucy takes a job as a secretary at a law firm and is horrible at it so they demote her to process serving. So she has to serve a subpoena to Mr. Mooney!

episodes.title: Lucy Enters a Baking Contest
episodes.desc: When Vivian makes some comments about Lucy's baking ability, Lucy enters a baking contest to prove her otherwise.  Lucy then swaps hers and Vivian's entries in the contest, which leads to a food fight!

3.description: The Lucy-Viv partnership reaches its final full season, with the series increasingly relying on elaborate physical set pieces and guest-driven episodes. Appearances by performers such as Jack Benny and George Burns help expand the show's scope, while Ball and Vance continue to anchor the comedy through sheer timing and familiarity. Even as the format begins to strain, the season preserves the last stretch of one of television's most effective comedic pairings.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Good Skate
episodes.desc: Wanting to spend more time with her daughter, Lucy & Chris go roller skating.  But when Lucy gets home, she can't get the skates off because her feet had swollen, and she has to wear them to a dance that night.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Plumber
episodes.desc: Mr. Mooney approves of Lucy hiring a plumber to fix her kitchen, and the one who shows up looks exactly like Jack Benny (and his assistant bears resemblance to Bob Hope).

episodes.title: Lucy and the Winter Sports
episodes.desc: Lucy is participated in Winter Sports in Skiing, Ice Hockey, Curling & Ice Skating. Vivian coached her to do it when a true athlete to please help out in the forthcoming Olympics. She can win a decathlon for the bank.

episodes.title: Lucy Gets Amnesia
episodes.desc: Lucy got a hit on the head and can't remember everything. Viv escorts her to the doctor for help out the symptom. After all the forgetfulness... Lucy's Memory is Restored.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Great Bank Robbery
episodes.desc: Lucy & Mr. Mooney believes in the Bank Robbery by Crooks to ""Stick 'em UP!"" Lucy will attacks the crooks before they steal the assets of the bank and that makes a heroine.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Meter Maid
episodes.desc: When Lucy's new job as a meter maid goes to her head, she accuses Viv of many violations and takes her to traffic court.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Coin Collector
episodes.desc: When Jerry finds a penny worth fifty cents, Lucy & Viv withdraw 2,000 pennies from the bank, and search through them until they find one worth $16.50.  When Lucy drops the penny down a storm drain, they disguise themselves as workmen, and climb their way through the sewer system searching for it.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Missing Stamp
episodes.desc: While trying to sell Mr. Mooney a vacuum cleaner, Lucy accidentally vacuums up a stamp Mr. Mooney had just bought for $3,000.  When the stamp winds up on an envelope sent through the mail, Lucy breaks into the post office to get it back.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Ceramic Cat
episodes.desc: When Lucy and Viv break the ceramic cat Mr. Mooney plans to give his wife as a surprise birthday present, Lucy winds up pretending to be a mechanical man in a department store window to steal another ceramic cat.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Countess Lose Weight
Lucy and Arthur Godfrey
episodes.desc: Lucy & Viv are able to talk Arthur Godfrey to appear in a local musical they and their community actors are putting on.  Lucy plays Lucy Bell, Mr. Mooney plays the millionaire, and Vivian plays Steamboat Bessie.

episodes.title: Lucy the Stockholder
episodes.desc: When Lucy and Vivian try to get the town's wealthiest doctor to become a depositor in Mr. Mooney's bank, the doctor, mistaking the girls for volunteers for an experiment in hypnosis, regressing them to the age of five.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Disc Jockey
episodes.desc: Lucy wins a contest to be disc jockey for a day, and proceeds to wreak typical, Lucy-style havoc in the radio studio.

4.description: With Vivian Vance departing as a regular and the show relocating fully to California, the series resets around Lucy's job at the bank under Mr. Mooney. The Ball–Gordon pairing becomes the defining dynamic, built on escalating frustration and perfectly timed reversals. This transitional season sharpens the show's identity, trading the two-woman premise for a more focused comedic engine while maintaining strong ratings within CBS's lineup.

episodes.title: Lucy at Marineland
episodes.desc: Lucy and her son Jerry arrive in LA (her daughter has gone to bording school, and Vivian has gotten married, it is explained), where, coincidentally, Mr. Mooney has also relocated to.  Lucy coerces Mr. Mooney into taking her and Jerry to Marine Land, where Lucy ultimately ends up in the pool with the dolphins.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Countess Have a Horse Guest
episodes.desc: Lucy babysits a neighbor's horse.

episodes.title: Lucy Helps Danny Thomas
episodes.desc: Mr. Mooney instructs Lucy to deliver some documents to Danny Thomas, and through a series of misunderstandings, she ends up performing in his musical act.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Return of the Iron Man
Lucy, the Choirmaster
episodes.desc: Lucy Carmichael's 1st Christmas in California. Part 1 has Lucy expressing determination not to spend too much on presents & Christmas tree in a typical tete a tete with a dubious Mr. Mooney. She succeeds in mixing up all of Mr. Mooney's gift tags for his wrapped Christmas presents. Part 2 takes place as Lucy    shops for a Christmas tree withe the scene ending with Lucy knocking lots of the inventory over with her new large tree. Part 3 has her recruit Mr. Mooney to sing bass with the children's choir at a charity event at the bank.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Rain Goddess
episodes.desc: Lucy sets out to track down her boss, Mr. Mooney, who is enjoying a secret vacation at a dude ranch. Lucy's mission is to obtain Mr. Mooney's signature on some important documents.  However, she mistakenly finds herself on an Indian reservation, where she bears a striking resemblance to the rain goddess depicted on the tribe's totem pole.

episodes.title: Lucy and Art Linkletter
episodes.desc: As a contestant on Art Linkletter's television show, Lucy is challenged to remain silent for 24 hours, with the promise of a $200 reward if she succeeds. However, Linkletter sends another audience member named Ruth, who is actually a plant from the show, to get her to break her silence.

episodes.title: Lucy Bags a Bargain
episodes.desc: Lucy takes on a part-time job at a department store to earn money for a dinette set she intends to purchase. However, her day at the store becomes a series of comedic misadventures.

episodes.title: Lucy Meets Mickey Rooney
episodes.desc: Mickey Rooney comes to the bank to secure a loan to purchase an acting school, and finds himself inadvertently enrolling his loan officers, Mr. Mooney and Lucy, as his students. As the three of them embark on their acting adventure, the episode pays homage to silent movie comedies.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Soap Opera
episodes.desc: Lucy takes matters into her own hands when her favorite actor friend faces being written out of the beloved soap opera, Camden Cove. Determined to help her friend, Lucy employs various disguises and strategies to influence the outcome of a pivotal trial in the show.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes to a Hollywood Premiere
episodes.desc: Lucy, an avid movie fan, seizes an opportunity to attend a major Hollywood movie premiere by posing as a theater usher. Her love for films and celebrities leads her to create chaos as the stars arrive on the red carpet.  Her overzealous behavior captures the attention of the crowd and draws the ire of some of the stars... and a gorilla.

episodes.title: Lucy Dates Dean Martin
episodes.desc: Lucy is set up on a date with Dean Martin's stunt double.  When the double can't make it, Dean himself substitutes on the date.  During the date Lucy keeps telling him how bad it is that Dean Martin is stealing all of his glory.

episodes.title: Lucy and Bob Crane
episodes.desc: Bob Crane visits the bank where Lucy works to open an account. However, their encounter takes an unexpected turn when Lucy ends up securing a dinner date with him.  Coincidentally, the bank has invested in Bob's upcoming World War I film, and Lucy's boss, Mr. Mooney, happens to know the director. Mr. Mooney, for his own amusement and to irritate Lucy, guarantees that Iron Man Carmichael, a stuntman, will perform stunts for the movie.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Robot
episodes.desc: Lucy finds herself assisting an inventor friend in obtaining funding for his projects. To achieve this, she comes up with a plan involving Mr. Mooney, her boss. Mr. Mooney agrees to provide financial support if Lucy and her friend can help him deal with his troublesome 13-year-old nephew.

episodes.title: Lucy and Clint Walker
episodes.desc: Lucy takes it upon herself to surprise her boyfriend, Frank, by knitting him a sweater. Secretly taking his measurements, she stays up all night diligently crafting a red sweater for him. However, her efforts result in an oversized creation that could easily cover a car. To make matters worse, Lucy discovers that Frank despises the color red.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Gun Moll
episodes.desc: Federal agent Stack discovers that Lucy is a dead ringer for gangster Gordon's moll and gets her to go undercover in order to recover missing bank robbery loot.  This episode was a spoof of the classic crime drama The Untouchables which was produced and filmed at Desilu Studios.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Superwoman
episodes.desc: When a heavy computer accidentally falls on Mr. Mooney's foot, with a burst of adrenaline Lucy finds herself able to lift the massive machine on her own, exhibiting superhuman strength. Surprisingly, her adrenal gland remains "on," leaving her with an ongoing power that grants her incredible abilities.  However, Lucy's newfound strength comes with a downside. Her superhuman power proves to be uncontrollable, causing unintentional destruction wherever she goes.

5.description: Now fully centered on Lucy's battles with Mr. Mooney, the series leans into larger set pieces and celebrity-driven episodes while remaining a strong presence in CBS's weekly lineup. A standout appearance by John Wayne places Lucy in a Hollywood setting that plays off his screen persona, while the season also introduces a travel arc with “Lucy Flies to London,” in which Lucy wins a trip abroad through a jingle contest. That storyline carries directly into the CBS special Lucy in London, giving the season a rare piece of continuity beyond the standard episodic format. The result is a run that balances its workplace foundation with broader, event-driven storytelling as Lucille Ball's television presence continues to evolve.

episodes.title: Lucy and George Burns
episodes.desc: George Burns arrives at the bank to pick up his statement, but Mr. Mooney has problems locating it.  While waiting for Lucy to return to work, Mooney and Burns chat, and Mr. Burns reveals that he's having trouble finding another girl to be in his act.  Enter Lucy, who goes on to explain her rather unique filing system, impressing George so much that he asks her to be his new comedy partner.  The two go on to do a hit show and are offered a gig in Las Vegas, but Lucy declines, saying that she could never leave Mr. Mooney behind.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Submarine
episodes.desc: When Lucy accidentally gets Mr. Mooney stationed on a submarine by talking too much, she sneaks onto the submarine dressed as a sailor to get him to sign some important contracts, but the submarine takes off before Lucy can exit.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Bean Queen
episodes.desc: Lucy finds a sale on beans that says ""The Best Beans You've Ever Eaten or Double Your Money Back"".  Needing money for new furniture, Lucy & Mary Jane keep buying and returning more and more beans until they have collected $1,500.00!  Little does Lucy know that Mr. Mooney is helping to sell them.

episodes.title: Lucy and Paul Winchell
episodes.desc: Ventriloquist Paul Winchell comes into the bank and opens an account with Lucy.  When Mr. Mooney fails to find a celebrity for an upcoming benefit for the bank, Lucy suggests that Mr. Winchell appear.  But a mishap causes Lucy to have to stand in as the ventriloquist's dummy on the night of the show.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Ring-a-Ding-Ding
episodes.desc: Mr. Mooney has a $6,000 ring delivered, which is an anniversary present for his wife.  Lucy wants the thrill of having such an expensive rock on her finger for a brief moment, and Mooney obliges, only to find that the ring won't come off.  Lucy reveals that she ""has a condition"" that causes her fingers to swell when she becomes nervous, so she takes some relaxing pills that the doctor prescribed her.  But the pills make her loopy and knock her out, so Mr. Mooney spends the remainder of the day dragging Lucy's body around and trying to get her to wake up long enough to get the ring off. He finally gets her conscious, only to end up with the ring lost in the drain.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes to London
episodes.desc: Lucy writes a jingle for a dog food company and wins a trip to London, England (""in Europe"").  But when she misses her flight, she's forced to take a commuting flight to New York -- in a seat next to Mr. Mooney, who's on his way to the Big Apple for a business meeting.

episodes.title: Lucy Gets a Roommate
episodes.desc: Lucy advertises for a roommate to help with the expenses, and after reading the correspondence, she finally decides on Carol Bradford (Carol Burnet), a frumpy off-kilter librarian.  Carol soon starts taking over, reorganizing the apartment, laughing like a hyena,  and generally driving Lucy nuts.  Lucy and Mary Jane scheme to help Carol develop a social life (to keep her out of Lucy's hair), so they have a small party to introduce her to some boys.  It isn't long before usually mild-mannered Carol begins drinking, singing, and being the life of the party.

episodes.title: Lucy and Carol in Palm Springs
episodes.desc: Lucy fakes sick so she can get out of work and accompany Carol, who's going to entertain at a golf tournament in Palm Springs.  When they arrive, the hotel staff realizes that there's two women rooming together, so Lucy finds herself having to partake in the show.  Lucy later weasels her way into an encounter with a distinguished actor, Collin Grant (Dan Rowan), and gets herself invited to a dinner dance.  It's a good excuse not to go back to work when she finds that Mr. Mooney is Mr. Grant's partner in the golf tournament...

episodes.title: Lucy Gets Caught Up in the Draft
episodes.desc: When a letter arrives from the US Government, Lucy finds that she's mistakenly been drafted.  Instead of ""Lucy,"" the letter is addressed to ""Lou C.""  So she sets off to the draft office to correct the mistake, but due to stupid ""rules and regulations,"" Lucy finds that she has to become a marine until the paperwork gets straightened out...  So she does what any woman would do -- she makes life miserable for her Sargent.

episodes.title: Lucy and John Wayne
episodes.desc: When Mr. Mooney sends Lucy to the movie studio to deliver some papers regarding the financing on John Wayne's new film, she ignores his warning not to bother Mr. Wayne and finds herself lunching with him.  Nervous and excited, Lucy proceeds to talk his ear off and finally splatters him with ketchup.  Mr. Wayne takes that as his exit cue, but Lucy soon tracks him to the set of his film, where she continuously screws up the production.

episodes.title: Lucy and Pat Collins
episodes.desc: Lucy and Mary Jane spend their lunch hour shopping for furs (despite the fact they only have 27 cents between them) so they can take advantage of the store's free tea and sandwiches.  As they're getting ready to leave, the duo run into Pat Collins, a hypnotist, who says she's appearing at The Royal Club.  Later at the bank, Mr. Mooney confides to Lucy that he has insomnia and hasn't slept in days, so she offers to take him to see Pat to cure him of his sleep deprivation.  But Mr. Mooney has his reservations about being hypnotized, so he drags Lucy on stage with him...

episodes.title: Lucy and the Monkey
episodes.desc: Lucy is overworked, between the work that she's neglected and having to sell tickets for a benefit for the bank.  Mary Jane warns that Lucy will have a nervous breakdown if she's not careful, but Lucy initially blows off this comment -- but she later thinks she's been hallucinating when she enters Mr. Mooney's office and finds a monkey, who's part of the benefit show,  sitting at Mooney's desk.  Lucy thinks she's been seeing Mr. Mooney as a monkey, so it's off to the psychiatrist's office, who gives her this advice: ""Face your fear and it will disappear.""  So on her next encounter with the simian, she treats him as if he's Mooney... which gets confusing as Mooney and the monkey simultaneously enter and exit the room...

episodes.title: Lucy and Phil Silvers
episodes.desc: Efficiency expert Oliver Kasten takes over Mr. Mooney's office and commandeers his secretary, Lucy Carmichael, who quickly patterns herself after Kasten, following his strict orders without question.

episodes.title: Lucy's Substitute Secretary
episodes.desc: Lucy is preparing to go on vacation, but she has a change of heart when her substitute secretary turns out to be an attractive blonde who Mr. Mooney really likes.  Lucy secretly doesn't go on vacation, and spies on them in various disguises.

episodes.title: Viv Visits Lucy
episodes.desc: Recently married Vivian leaves Danfield  to visit Lucy and the two begin gossiping and making up for lost time.  When Viv tells Lucy about a friend's son who's now sporting long-hair, a guitar and a clock earring, the duo decide to dress up as hippies and set off to Sunset Strip to find ""Itchy"" to rescue him from the underworld.  Lucy and Viv quickly find themselves in The Hairy Ape, where they finally find Itchy entertaining â€“ after they spend the evening having to dance with the hip crowd.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Baby Sitter
episodes.desc: Wanting a break from the bank and to make some extra money, Lucy visits an employment agency and lands a job as a babysitter.  Little does she know that the babies are actually chimps!

episodes.title: Main Street U.S.A.
episodes.desc: Lucy holds a protest against a freeway destroying a small town.  The catch is that Mr. Mooney is helping to get the freeway.

episodes.title: Lucy Puts Main Street on the Map
episodes.desc: Picking up where ""Main Street USA"" left off, Lucy, Mr. Mooney, and Mel Tinker stage a shootout at the bank for press coverage, to bring the freeway problem to the attention of the world.

episodes.title: Lucy Meets the Law
episodes.desc: Lucy is mistaken for a shoplifter called ""The Red Flash"" and taken to jail, but she thinks she's in jail because she littered a candy wrapper.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Fight Manager
episodes.desc: Mr. Mooney has a fight with his wife, so Lucy decides to have some flowers sent over for her.  When Eddie, the florist, arrives with the arrangement, Mr. Mooney immediately recognizes him as a former boxer.  Eddie wants $3,000 to open up his own florist shop, but Mr. Mooney denies him a loan â€“ so Lucy arranges for Eddie to partake in a boxing match to earn the money.

episodes.title: Lucy and Tennessee Ernie Ford
episodes.desc: Mr. Cheever warns Mr. Mooney that his job is in jeopardy unless he attracts new business to their branch of the bank.  So when Lucy hears that a recording star, country hick Homer Higgins (Ernie Ford), is about to become a California resident, she sets off to the hotel to coax him into setting up an account with their bank.  To impress Mr. Higgins and his family, Lucy and Mr. Mooney set up a hoe-down in the bank after it closes that evening...and Lucy decides to dress down to pose as Mrs. Mooney.

episodes.title: Lucy Meets Sheldon Leonard
episodes.desc: When Sheldon Leonard convinces Mr. Mooney to let him shoot a televison pilot at the bank, Lucy mistakingly thinks he is a gangster who plans to rob the bank, so she and Mary Jane sneak into the bank at night, disguised as scrubwomen, to stop them.

6.description: The final season doubles down on the guest-star formula, bringing in high-profile names including Joan Crawford and Carol Burnett, along with a return appearance from Vivian Vance that reconnects the series to its earlier era. The structure is looser, with episodes often built around celebrity interactions and extended comic setups, but Ball's performance remains the anchor. The show continues to draw strong ratings for CBS, closing out its run as one of the defining sitcoms of the decade.

episodes.title: Lucy Meets the Berles
episodes.desc: Needing some extra money, Lucy takes a part-time job as the secretary for Milton Berle.  While Working for Mr. Berle, she suspects that he is having an affair and does the best she can to stop him in his tracks.  (This synopsis is from the Front Row Features DVD.)

episodes.title: Lucy Gets Trapped
episodes.desc: Lucy fakes sickness to go shopping at a one-day-only sale with Mary Jane.  But when Lucy becomes the customer of the year and has her photo printed in the paper, Lucy must hide the paper from Mr. Mooney before he sees it.

episodes.title: Lucy and the French Movie Star
episodes.desc: Whe famous French movie star Jacques DuPre expresses interest in opening an account at Mr. Mooney's bank, Lucy is asked to visit his apartment and dictate a formal letter of agreement confirming the account.  While visiting, she drinks a little too much champagne and makes a complete fool of herself.  But Monsieur DuPre gets a kick out of it, and gets the account anyway.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Starmaker
episodes.desc: Mr. Cheever's wisecracking nephew, Tommy (Frankie Avalon), comes to work at the bank, to discourage him from the position he really wants â€“ a job in show business.  Despite the fact that he's to get no special treatment, Mooney repeatedly sucks up to him.  Lucy soon befriends the young Cheever, who impresses her with a song and dance.  So Lucy sets out to break Tommy into show biz...

episodes.title: Lucy Gets Her Diploma
episodes.desc: Lucy is in trouble when a new bank policy requires all of its employees be high school graduates, and she reveals she was unable to complete her final year.  So now it's back to school for Lucy, who ends up helping another man earn his diploma as well.

episodes.title: Lucy and Jack Benny's Account
episodes.desc: Needing new accounts for the bank, Lucy visits Jack Benny to try and get him to keep his money at her bank.  He agrees to do it if she can prove that her bank can build a vault safer than his.  Lucy then has the bank build a burglar proof vault, and gives Mr. Benny a personal tour of it.

episodes.title: Little Old Lucy
episodes.desc: Lucy and Mr. Mooney are sent to pick up Mr. Heatherington, the wealthy bank president, from the airport, and the gentleman finds that Lucy strikes his fancy.  So when Mr. Mooney neglects to find a lady for Mr. Heatherington to escort to that evening's banquet, Lucy is given the job.  But to her dismay, she's forced to dress up as a little old lady.  As it turn's out, little old Lucy strikes perverse Mr. Heatherington's fancy more than the younger Lucy...

episodes.title: Lucy and Robert Goulet
episodes.desc: When a poor trucker Chuck Willis(Robert Goulet) gets turned down by the bank for a loan, Lucy decides to help him out by entering him in a Robert Goulet lookalike contest.  When he loses the contest, Lucy decides to take matters into her own hands.

episodes.title: Lucy and Carol Burnett (1)
episodes.desc: Lucy and Carol (Carol Burnett) become flight-attendant trainees. Part 1 of two. Lucille Ball. Brennan: Rhodes Reason. Miss Cavanaugh: Kasey Rogers. Workman: Sid Gould.

episodes.title: Lucy and Carol Burnett (2)
episodes.desc: Conclusion. Lucy and Carol (Carol Burnett) do a musical salute to aviation. Actors Richard Arlen and Buddy Rogers appear together for the first time since the 1927 film "Wings."

episodes.title: Lucy and Viv Reminisce
episodes.desc: Lucy's old pal Viv Bunson comes out west to care for Lucy after she breaks her leg.  They spend time looking back at when they lived together, until Viv breaks her leg while on her way to fix lunch, forcing Mr. Mooney to look after both of them.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Pool Hustler
episodes.desc: Lucy visits a local pool hall and enters their billiards tournament. Her main competition is a woman named Laura Winthrop. Cigar-smoking Laura, however, looks suspiciously like a a pool hustling man named Ace.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Lost Star
episodes.desc: Lucy & Viv's car breaks down, so they visit the nearest house for help, which happens to belong to Joan Crawford.  All of Crawford's furniture is away being redone, which lead the women to suspect she is broke, so they ask Mr. Mooney to help get her back to work.

episodes.title: Lucy and The Boss of the Year Award
episodes.desc: When Mr. Mooney is up for becoming president of the San Fransisco branch of the bank, Lucy tries to get him named Boss of the Year so he gets the position.

1.description: Lucille Ball returns as Lucy Carter, a widowed secretary working for her brother-in-law Harry at an employment agency in Los Angeles. The premise blends the workplace structure of The Lucy Show with a more explicit family setup, incorporating her real-life children while keeping the focus squarely on Lucy herself. The central dynamic with Gale Gordon remains the engine, their familiar rhythm adapting easily to the new setting. Early on, the show leans on situations rather than spectacle, reestablishing Ball's presence on CBS with a format that feels recognizable but slightly updated.

episodes.title: Mod, Mod Lucy
episodes.desc: Cheapskate Uncle Harry hire's his nephew & neice, Craig and Kim, as the band needed by a client at the Unique Employment Agency.  Lead vocalist Kim develops laryngitis, however, and mom Lucy, decked out in modern fashions,  fills in for her.

episodes.title: Lucy Visits Jack Benny
episodes.desc: The Carter family vacations in Jack Benny's desert mansion.  Percieved as a budget getaway, Benny loads charge after charge to up the bill.  This exceptional episode has a cameo by Jackie Gleason playing his classic Ralph Kramden character from the Honeymooners.

episodes.title: Lucy the Process Server
episodes.desc: Lucy confuses a bank deposit and a summons.  Amusing episode borrowing on older Lucille Ball plots.

episodes.title: Lucy and Miss Shelly Winters
episodes.desc: A movie producer is worried when an actress' pre-filming jitters is subdued by over-eating.  Lucy, after contacted at the Unique Employeement Agency, moves in with actress Shelly to help her with a crash diet.

episodes.title: Lucy the Conclusion Jumper
episodes.desc: Lucy jumps to the wrong conclusion!  Daughter Lucie is working on a  school project about teenage marriage, and Lucy fears she is about to elope.

episodes.title: Lucy's Impossible Mission
episodes.desc: The first of several episodes of Here's Lucy that has her working undercover.  This episode is based on the long running Desilu tv show, Mission Impossible, and features the Carter family impersonating Middle Eastern royalty.

episodes.title: Lucy and Eva Gabor
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry sends screenplay author Eva Von Kirsten to hide out at Lucy's home as a safe haven to complete her latest work.  As usual, Lucy takes her job very seriously.

episodes.title: Lucy's Birthday
episodes.desc: The kids take Lucy out for a birthday dinner.  Unfortunately, Craig forgets to bring his money, and Lucy schemes to get out of paying the bill.

episodes.title: Lucy Sells Craig to Wayne Newton
episodes.desc: Lucy schemes to get Craig a job as Wayne Newton's drummer.

episodes.title: Lucy's Working Daughter
episodes.desc: Over protective mom Lucy watches over Lucie at her new after school job.

episodes.title: Guess Who Owes Lucy $23.50?
episodes.desc: Unoriginal Van Johnson episode where Lucy Carter uses a Van Johnson look=alike (played by Johnson, of course) to masquerade as the movie star.

episodes.title: Lucy the Matchmaker
episodes.desc: Lucy uses a computer dating service--for Uncle Harry.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Gold Rush
episodes.desc: Lucy discovers gold?  Of course not, but she thinks so and the gold rush begins.

episodes.title: Lucy the Fixer
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry requires Lucille to work on Saturday at his house.  By the end of the day, his house is a shambles.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Ex-Con
episodes.desc: Lucy goes undercover (as an old lady) to catch the crook of a robbery she may have caused.  Wally Cox guest stars as a security guard.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes on Strike
episodes.desc: Lucy thinks Harry is trying to murder her!?!  Even worse than the 1st episode of I Love Lucy in 1951 in which Lucy thinks Ricky is trying to murder her!

episodes.title: Lucy and Carol Burnett
episodes.desc: One of Lucille Ball's variety shows, and with Carol Burnette to boot!

episodes.title: Lucy and the Great Airport Chase
episodes.desc: Waaaayyy out there episode involving secret formulas, suicide, secret agents, and a keystone cops style chase scene.

episodes.title: A Date for Lucy
episodes.desc: The kids fix Mom Lucy up with a latin hunk who turns out to be a jewel thief.

episodes.title: Lucy the Shopping Expert
episodes.desc: Family oriented episode revolving around the supermarket.

episodes.title: Lucy Gets Her Man
episodes.desc: Lucy must go undercover . . . huh, wait. . . that was episode numbers . . . . no, no this time she's a spy.

episodes.title: Lucy's Safari
episodes.desc: Lucy and family try to catch a Gorboona.

episodes.title: Lucy and Tennessee Ernie's Fun Farm
episodes.desc: Stale episode rehashing old Tennessee Ernie/Lucy plots from her previous series-hillbilly music and down on his luck Ernie.

episodes.title: Lucy Helps Craig Get a Driver's License
episodes.desc: Craig celebrates his 16th birthday.  Naturally, he can't wait to get his drivers license.  Mom Lucille naturally interferes.

2.description: The structure settles into a dependable pattern built around the agency and Lucy's attempts to bend it to her will. Guest stars begin to play a larger role, with appearances by Ginger Rogers and Ann-Margret folded directly into the storylines. These episodes are less about narrative complexity and more about placing Lucy alongside recognizable personalities and letting the contrast carry the comedy. The formula works, though it begins to suggest where the show will increasingly rely for variety.

episodes.title: Lucy Joins the Air Force Academy (1)
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry is hired to drive a motor home from California to Colorado, and foolishly agrees to take Lucy and the kids along for the ride.  Lucy takes over driving so Harry can take a nap, and decides to take a detour, setting up this and the following three episodes.

episodes.title: Lucy Joins the Air Force Academy (2)
episodes.desc: Lucy is still trying as hard as she can to get Craig into the Air Force Academy, and each result ends hilariously.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Indian Chief
episodes.desc: Lucy indadvertently ends up marrying an Indian Chief who gives her the state of Utah as a wedding gift!

episodes.title: Lucy Runs the Rapids
episodes.desc: While camping outdoors near a river, Lucy's mattress gets carried away with the current, with Kim, Craig, and Harry trying to rescue her.

episodes.title: Lucy and Harry's Tonsils
episodes.desc: Lucy, Kim, and Craig attempt to keep Uncle Harry in the hospital after having his tonsils taken out.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Andrews Sisters
episodes.desc: Lucy and Kim fill in for two of Patty Andrews' Sisters

episodes.title: Lucy's Burglar Alarm
episodes.desc: After a rash of neighborhood burglaries,  Lucille, Kim, and Craig install an home-made alarm system.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Used Car Dealer
episodes.desc: Needing another means of transportation, Kim and Craig buy an old broken down car from Cheerful Charlie, the local car salesman.  When the car no longer works, Lucy and Harry scheme to get Charlie to take it back for more than it's worth.

episodes.title: Lucy the Cement Worker
episodes.desc: When Lucille loses an antique ring in wet cement, she disguises herself as a cement worker wielding a jackhammer.

episodes.title: Lucy and Johnny Carson
episodes.desc: Lucy appears on the Johnny Carson show and plays ""stump the band"", and wins a dinner at the Brown Derby.  Unfortunately for Johnny Carson,  Lucy and Uncle Harry coincidentally dine at the Brown Derby when Carson and McMahon are also dining.  Naturally, Lucy believes she must have won a dinner with Johnny and Ed and joins them.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Generation Gap
episodes.desc: Kim and Craig are in charge of producing the school play, and decide its theme should be the Generation Gap.  When they have casting problems, Lucy and Harry wind up playing the parents, and they all put on a really spectacular show.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Bogie Affair
episodes.desc: The kids find a lost sheep dog and bring it home.  After naming the sheep dog Humphrey Bogart, a litter of puppies are born.  After the puppies are giving to new homes, Uncle Harry arrives with a want ad offering a reward.

episodes.title: Lucy Protects Her Job
episodes.desc: Harry suggests Lucy is overworked and should hire an assistant.  Suspecting Harry is really attempting to replace her, Lucy has Kim arrive to the office as temp secretary Shoiley Shopenauer, the world's worst office assistant.

episodes.title: Lucy the Helpful Mother
episodes.desc: The kids acquire part-time jobs to pay for a new phone line, but Lucy ends up filling in for them.

episodes.title: Lucy and Liberace
episodes.desc: Craig's scavenger hunt finds a candlelabra that appears to have belonged to Liberace.  Lucy feels obliged to return it to the famous pianist.

episodes.title: Lucy the Laundress
episodes.desc: When Lucy's drives into a Chinese Laundry, she takes a part time job there to pay for the damages.

episodes.title: Lucy and Lawrence Welk
episodes.desc: Lucy brags to Viv that she knows Lawrence Welk personally, and then schemes to introduce her to the bandleader, whom she has actually never met.

episodes.title: Lucy and Viv Visit Tijuana
episodes.desc: Lucy and her friend Vivian Jones want to visit Tijuana, and get Harry to drive them, which leads to another classic Lucy-Viv situation.

episodes.title: Lucy and Ann-Margret
episodes.desc: Ann-Margret likes Desi's new song, and wants to duet with him on her upcoming television show.

episodes.title: Lucy and Wally Cox
episodes.desc: Lucy schemes to help change a father's image of his son.

episodes.title: Lucy and Wayne Newton
episodes.desc: While in Las Vegas, Lucy and family return a lost horse to singer Wayne Newton.

episodes.title: Lucy Takes Over
episodes.desc: Lucy, Kim, and Craig find an old promisory note indicating Uncle Harry owes them a great deal of money.

episodes.title: Lucy and Carol Burnett
episodes.desc: Lucy and her friend Carol Krausmeyer both end up entering the local Secretary Beautiful Contest, causing a rivalry between them.  They both make fools of themselves at the contest trying to prove which woman is better.

3.description: The guest-driven approach peaks with the season's opening episode featuring Elizabeth Taylor and Richard Burton, built around the well-publicized diamond ring sequence. It became one of the most-watched broadcasts of the era and set the template for what the show would return to: high-profile names, simple premises, and extended comic situations built around Lucy's persistence. The season still holds together, but the balance has clearly shifted away from tightly constructed farce toward broader setups.

episodes.title: Lucy Meets the Burtons
episodes.desc: Richard Burton, the movie star, escapes riotous fans by wearing a plumber's disguise.  Lucy Carter mistakes him for a much needed plumber.  Sam (Burton), the plumber, escapes to the Carter household.  Lucy later finds a remarkable diamond ring in his discarded over-alls.  By the time Lucy discovers it is THE famous Elizabeth Taylor diamond ring, it is already stuck on her finger.  The end result is a creshendo of comedy as Liz feverishly tries to get the ring off Lucy's finger.  Ultimately, Lucy's arms are used as Liz Taylor's while Richard & Liz do a scheduled  press conference.

episodes.title: Lucy the Skydiver
episodes.desc: Lucy threatens to take up skydiving if her kids don't drop their dangerous activities, but Harry convinces her to actually do it as publicity for the Employment Agency.

episodes.title: Lucy and Sammy Davis, Jr.
episodes.desc: Lucy ends up hitting Sammy Davis Jr. while opening the door to the employment agency and it hits his nose which causes him to threaten to sue Harry and Lucy if his nose looks different for the movie he is filming.

episodes.title: Lucy the Crusader
episodes.desc: When Lucy buys Craig a record player for his birthday it ends up not working right, so Lucy brings it back to the store but they won't accept it back which causes Lucy and other victims of the companies defective products to present their case in front of shareholders.

episodes.title: Lucy, the American Mother
episodes.desc: Craig produces a documentary focusing on his family, who become self-conscious of the camera.

episodes.title: Lucy's Wedding Party
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry is away, so Lucy borrows his home for a Greek couple's wedding . . . .

episodes.title: Lucy, the Diamond Cutter
episodes.desc: Lucy attempts to sooth the nerves of a nervous diamond cutter.

episodes.title: Lucy and Jack Benny's Biography
episodes.desc: Harry sends Lucy to Jack Benny's house where she writes down his biography.  Important and significant events throughout his life like him as a child practicing violin to being in the navy to shows with Gracie Allen.

episodes.title: Lucy Loses Her Cool
episodes.desc:
Lucy is chosen on an audience participation game show.  The host offers her $500 if she won't lose her temper for a week.

When she's escorted off the stage, the host then brings out Harry & the kids, offering them $500 if they can make Lucy lose her temper!

They do everything possible to make Lucy lose, but she overcomes temptation.  Lucy manages to demolish a grocery store!

The week passed.  Lucy's back on the game show where the host awards her $500.  But then, the manager of the grocery store enters & charges her $499.99 in damages, leaving her with 1 penny!  Lucy gets mad & cries until the host reveals that her friends/family were in this charade & award her another $500!

4.description: By this point, the show is operating on a familiar rhythm. The Lucy–Harry relationship continues to provide structure, with Gale Gordon's controlled frustration grounding increasingly broad premises. Guest appearances remain frequent, but they often function more as a framework for Lucy's antics than as fully integrated story elements. The series remains effective in individual moments, though it leans more heavily on established patterns than on new variations.

episodes.title: Lucy and Flip Go Legit
episodes.desc: Lucy convinces Flip Wilson to play the part of Prissy in a local production of Gone With the Wind, with Harry as Rhett Butler, Lucy as Scarlett O'Hara, and Kim as Melanie.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Mountain Climber
episodes.desc: Lucy bets Harry's business partner that she can beat him at mounting climbing.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Italian Bombshell
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry's ex-flame Donna is coming to town, and Harry begins to worry about his appearance.

episodes.title: Lucy and Mannix Are Held Hostage
episodes.desc: Lucy hires detective Joe Mannix.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Astronauts
episodes.desc: Lucy must go through decontamination with Astronauts when she makes physical contact with them after a landing.

episodes.title: Lucy Makes a Few Extra Dollars
episodes.desc: Lucy and Kim attempt to make Uncle Harry guilty for not giving Lucy a pay raise.

episodes.title: Someone's On the Ski Lift with Dinah
episodes.desc: Lucy encounters Dinah Shore while on a ski trip. The two get stuck together on a ski lift, where Lucy proceeds to sing a medley of Dinah's hits to her, and otherwise annoy the singer.

episodes.title: Lucy and Her All-Nun Band
episodes.desc: Lucy takes saxophone lessons to join a band.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Celebrities
episodes.desc: Lucy and Uncle Harry seek celebrities for a radio campaign when a competitor makes a dent in their business.  Kim's boyfriend is an impressionist (Rich Little), and offers his services.

episodes.title: Won't You Calm Down, Dan Dailey?
episodes.desc: Lucy goes to work for movie star Dan Dailey, who pleads with Uncle Harry to take her back at the Unique Employment Agency.

episodes.title: Ginger Rogers Comes to Tea
episodes.desc: Lucille and Uncle Harry attend a Ginger Rogers film festival. Ms. Rogers attends in disguise, and is seated next to ""you-know-who"".  When Lucille offends Ms. Rogers, she leaves the theater, but also leaves her purse.  Discovering the true identity to the stranger, Harry & Lucy plot to return the purse to meet her again.

episodes.title: Lucy Helps David Frost Go Night-Night
Lucy in the Jungle
episodes.desc: The family vacation in Africa.

episodes.title: Lucy and Candid Camera
episodes.desc: The family believes they are on Candid Camera, but in actuality, the are assisting a robbery.

episodes.title: Lucy's Lucky Day
episodes.desc: Lucy is a game show contestant.

episodes.title: Lucy's Bonus Bounces
episodes.desc: Lucy flips a psychiatrists advice to Harry that she needs a raise, thinking Uncle Harry has something else up his sleeve.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Little Old Lady
episodes.desc: When an old lady comes in the enployment agency looking for a job because she has nowhere to go, Lucy decides to let her stay at her house. When Mrs. Brady sells land to Harry, Kim tells them about a similar sounding scam which gives Lucy and Harry the idea to let the Mrs. Brady have a seance in which they dress up as Napoleon and Josephine to convince her to refund Harry his money.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Chinese Curse
episodes.desc: Lucy saves a man's life, and according to Chinese custom, he is indepted to her.  Lucy and Kim sheme to correct an annoying situation.

episodes.title: Lucy's Replacement
episodes.desc: Harry gets a new office computer, and finds Lucy obsolete.  Out of work Lucy get a new job and hates it!  Lucy and Kim scheme to make the computer obsolete.

episodes.title: Kim Moves Out
episodes.desc: Kim has decided it is time for her to move out of the house, but Lucy becomes an annoying mother hen.  Ultimately, Kim plans to teach Mother a lesson.

episodes.title: Lucy Sublets the Office
episodes.desc: Lucy is promoted to office manager, and looks for ways to save money.  Her solution is to sublet half the office to a toy maker.

episodes.title: Lucy's Punctured Romance
episodes.desc: Lucy has a new boyfriend, but Kim and Uncle Harry are worried.  The milkman doesn't help matters when he informs the pair the Lucy's boyfriend is a playboy.

episodes.title: With Viv As a Friend, Who Needs an Enemy?
episodes.desc: Viv shows up for a surprise visit, and tells Lucy she may stay in California for a year, but everything goes awry when Harry fires Lucy and hires Viv in her place, making Lucy furious.  After a few weeks, Harry can't stand Viv and Viv can't stand Harry.  Lucy comes up with a scheme to get Viv to quit, but Kim blabs the whole thing to Viv.  In the end, everything ends up back the way it was.

episodes.title: Kim Finally Cuts You-Know-Who's Apron Strings
episodes.desc: Kim moves into her own apartment (one managed by Lucy's brother) and deals with neighbors and lecherous men.

5.description: A real-life leg injury to Lucille Ball is written into the show, limiting her mobility for much of the season and subtly shifting the tone. The physical comedy becomes more contained, with greater reliance on dialogue and interaction, particularly with Gale Gordon, whose role becomes even more central. Guest appearances from figures like Flip Wilson and Joe Namath continue the show's established approach, though the reliance on outside personalities is more apparent. The season reflects both the durability of Ball's presence and the increasing effort required to keep the format fresh.

episodes.title: Lucy's Big Break
episodes.desc: Lucy Carter breaks her leg, and is in the hospital.  She is attracted to a doctor, and attempts to get his affection.

episodes.title: Lucy and Eva Gabor Are Hospital Roomies
episodes.desc: Lucy's new hospital mate (Eva Gabor) has broken her foot, and steals the attention from Lucy.

episodes.title: Harrison Carter, Male Nurse
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry plays nurse when Lucy arrives home from the hospital.

episodes.title: A Home Is Not An Office
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry is lost without Lucille, and moves the office to her house while she is recovering.

episodes.title: Lucy and Joe Namath
episodes.desc: Craig is home from college.  While playing tennis, a football coach suspects he may also make a great quarterback.  But can even Joe Namath convince Lucy this is best for Craig?

episodes.title: The Case of the Wreckless Wheelchair Driver
episodes.desc: Lucy is involved in a lawsuit when a young man is flattened by Lucy's wheelchair.  Kim proves the guy is faking.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Other Woman
episodes.desc: The milkman's wife thinks Lucy is having an affair with her husband.

episodes.title: Lucy and Petula Clark
episodes.desc: Lucy tricks Harry into sending her to be Petula Clark's ""Girl Friday"" to assist with a recording session.  Lucy's over protection of pregnant Clark nearly wrecks the entire session, but ends by becoming the new gimmick for her record, a human ""waa waa petal"".

episodes.title: Lucy and Jim Bailey
episodes.desc: Lucie and Jim Bailey perform for a Chamber of Commerce event.  Jim Bailey impersonates Phyllis Diller.

episodes.title: Lucy and Dirty Gertie
episodes.desc: After cleaning her fireplace, Lucy takes a crate of apples down to a neighbor.  She is mistaken for Dirty Gertie by a crook who enlists the homeless woman for good luck.  Lucy is asked to go undercover to held apprehend the criminal.

episodes.title: Lucy and Donny Osmond
episodes.desc: Lucy and Kim attend a Donny Osmond concert with cousin Patricia (Eve Plumb), who is crazy for the 14 year old singer.  Donny, spots Kim in the audience, and he falls in love at first sight.  Kim believes the flowers and letters are for Patricia, but soon discovers the mix-up.  Realizing Donny volunteered to perform at Kim's Charity function because of the mix-up, Kim feels obligated to set the record straight.

episodes.title: Lucy and Her Prince Charming
episodes.desc: A visiting latin Prince falls for Lucy.  Uncle Harry sees dollar signs and encourages her to marry him.

episodes.title: My Fair Buzzi
episodes.desc: In preparation for an audition, Kim's friend Ruth get a makeover from Lucy and Kim.  Unfortunately, the director wants a less attractive actress, much like Ruth before the makeover.  Nonetheless, all three get hired for a 1920's style production.

episodes.title: Lucy and the Group Encounter
episodes.desc: Mary Jane suggests Lucy and Harry go to group therapy to improve their working relationship.  Once in therapy, the psychiatrist suggest the two reverse roles to see how the other one feels.

episodes.title: Lucy Is Really In a Pickle
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry recruits Lucy and Kim to don pickle costumes and perform in a television commercial, but as filming is about to begin, Lucy is nowhere to be found, as she finds herself caught up in yet another Lucy-style mishap.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes on Her Last Blind Date
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry tries to marry off Lucy to a rich relative, Uncle Ben.  Once again, Lucy isn't intrested.

episodes.title: Lucy and Her Genuine Twimby
episodes.desc: While antique shopping, Lucy purchases an  antique reproduction.  The dealer, however, has made a mistake and sold Lucy an original, rare antique, and romances Lucy in hopes of retrieving the chair.

episodes.title: Lucy Goes to Prison
episodes.desc: Lucy goes undercover again, this time to prison.  Lucy attempts to get needed information from a cell mate, but has little luck until Lucy fills her with alcohol.

6.description: In its final season, the series holds to its formula with only minor adjustments. The structure is familiar, the guest-driven approach remains in place, and the Lucy–Harry dynamic continues to carry most of the material. There are still effective moments, particularly when episodes allow Ball to build a situation gradually rather than relying on spectacle, but the repetition is harder to ignore. The show closes its run as a continuation of Ball's long television presence rather than a reinvention of it, sustained by her timing and professionalism even as the format shows its age.

episodes.title: Lucy and Danny Thomas
episodes.desc: Lucy schemes with a starving artist to fake his death, hoping his paintings will begin to sell.

episodes.title: The Big Game
episodes.desc: Uncle Harry books OJ Simpson as a guest speaker for the Beverly Hills Chamber of Commerce.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Peacemaker
episodes.desc: Lucy attempts to resolve the marriage separation between Eydie Gorme and Steve Lawrence.

episodes.title: Lucy the Wealthy Widow
episodes.desc: Lucy schemes to borrow money from a bank to redecorate the Unique Employment Agency.

episodes.title: The Bow-Wow Boutique
episodes.desc: Lucy and Kim work on weekends at a pet store.

episodes.title: Lucy Gives Eddie Albert the Old Song and Dance
episodes.desc: Lucy is mistaken for a stalker.

episodes.title: Lucy's Tenant
episodes.desc: Lucy rents out Kim's room, but soon chooses to break the lease.

episodes.title: Lucy and Joan Rivers Do Jury Duty
episodes.desc: Lucy disagrees with other jurist in court, and holds up proceedings.

episodes.title: Tipsy Through the Tulips
episodes.desc: Lucy attempts to keep an author sober, but discovers he has booze hidden everywhere.

episodes.title: The Carters Meet Frankie Avalon
episodes.desc: Kim and Frankie Avalon imitate Sonny & Cher in a local competition.

episodes.title: Harry Catches Gold Fever
episodes.desc: The family searches for gold.

episodes.title: Lucy and Chuck Connors Have a Surprise Slumber Party
episodes.desc: Lucy is banished from her own home after it is rented by a movie production company.  When all seems clear, Lucy returns to find Chuck Connors in her bed.

episodes.title: Lucy Plays Cops and Robbers
episodes.desc: Lucy chairs a neighborhood watch program started due to a string of recent robberies.  Lucy runs amok.

episodes.title: Lucy is a Bird Sitter
episodes.desc: Lucy is enlisted by Harry to sit for a rare New Guinea Weewawk.  Unfortunately, Lucy looses the bird.

episodes.title: Meanwhile, Back at the Office
episodes.desc: Harry sells the agency, but regrets it.  Lucy schemes to get the agency back for Uncle Harry.

episodes.title: Lucy is N.G. as an R.N.
episodes.desc: Lucy must care for Uncle Harry, Kim, and Mary Jane at the same time.

episodes.title: Lucy, the Sheriff
episodes.desc: A small Montana town contacts Lucy, who's great grandmother was a former sheriff, to be honored during a week long festival.

episodes.title: Milton Berle is the Life of the Party
episodes.desc: Chastized for her boring parties, Lucy wins a celebrity charity auction, enlisting Milton Berle as a guest for her next party.

episodes.title: Mary Jane's Boyfriend
episodes.desc: Mary Jane finally catches a man.  Unfortunately, the new boyfriend makes a play for Lucy.

episodes.title: Lucy and Phil Harris Strike Up the Band
episodes.desc: Lucy gets minorities hired in a band, but upsets women's rights group for not hiring any female musicians . . . . . . guess who fills in as a female vocalist?!

episodes.title: Lucy Carter Meets Lucille Ball
episodes.desc: Lucy enters a Lucille Ball lookalike contest and meets Lucille Ball.

episodes.title: Where is My Wandering Mother Tonight?
episodes.desc: Spending the weekend with Kim, Lucy overhears her daughter complaining about her visit and leaves.

episodes.title: Lucy Fights the System
episodes.desc: When a middle aged waitress is fired solely based on her age, Lucy and Kim scheme to cause chaos for the restaurant manager.

1.description: Lucille Ball returns to series television for the first time in over a decade, this time on ABC rather than CBS, in a role that again places her at the center of a family-run business. She plays a widowed grandmother involved in her late husband's hardware store, working opposite Gale Gordon, whose presence provides a direct link back to her earlier series. The setup is familiar, built around Lucy inserting herself into situations that quickly spiral, but the format reflects the challenges of adapting her established style to a different era of television. The physical comedy remains present, though more measured, and the pacing leans heavily on dialogue and setup rather than escalation. The series struggled to connect with audiences and was pulled after eight episodes, making it the shortest run of Ball's television career. While it lacks the structural precision of her earlier work, it still reflects the core elements she had relied on for decades: timing, control of the scene, and a clear understanding of how to build a comic situation. The result is less a reinvention than a final extension of a formula that had defined her career, closing out her long run in television with a quieter, more constrained effort.

episodes.title: One Good Grandparent Deserves Another
episodes.desc: Curtis comes back from a Hawaiian vacation with the intention of moving in with his son, Ted, and his family.  He arrives just in time to learn that his son's mother-in-law and Curtis's new business partner Lucy has moved in with the family.  Crotchety Curtis is annoyed that not only does he have to put up with her shenanigans at work, but now at home as well.

episodes.title: Lucy Makes a Hit with John Ritter
episodes.desc: John Ritter is doing a play in town, and stops into the hardware store for some antique door handles. When Lucy injures his hands and foot, she insists that John stay with her.  After the lead actress drops out of the play, Lucy talks John into offering her the part of his love interest.

episodes.title: Love Among the Two-by-Fours
episodes.desc: A former flame of Lucy's, who is a business prospect for Curtis, comes into the store.  After he takes her out, Lucy decides that she does not share the same feelings for him that he has carried for her.

episodes.title: Lucy Gets Her Wires Crossed
episodes.desc: When a talk-show host comes into the store, Lucy talks him into inviting Curtis on for a Mr-Fix-It segment.  Curtis reluctantly agrees to let Lucy join him, at the urging of Becky.  But while gluing a lamp together, Lucy ends up gluing herself to Curtis. . .and both talk show hosts!

episodes.title: Lucy is a Sax Symbol
episodes.desc: While looking for things to put in the yard sale, Lucy happens across her old saxophone and encourages Becky to take up playing.  However, Becky finds playing the instrument unsatisfying, as does the annoyed Curtis.

episodes.title: Lucy Makes Curtis Byte the Dust
episodes.desc: Lucy and Curtis get a computer to help with the books at the store.  When she uses it to order a non-returnable fleet of merchandise, Lucy tries to get the bank to stop the check -- and accidentally misinforms them that Curtis has died.

episodes.title: Lucy, Legal Beagle
episodes.desc: While trying to pass off a Kevin's teddy bear as her own during a clean-up sale, to help avoid taunting from one of Kevin's friends, she sticks it in a bag.  The bag is then purchased with the bear inside, so Lucy puts up reward posters around town.  When the woman comes in to pick up the reward, she's asking for a lot more than the $50 that Lucy is offering.

episodes.title: Mother of the Bride
episodes.desc: Lucy's sister Audrey comes to town just in time to see Margo and Ted renew their vows.  Lucy's excitement is diminished when Audrey starts making all the wedding arrangements, resulting in a cake fight between the two siblings.

specials.title: The Lucille Ball Comedy Hour
specials.description: Lucille Ball plays the head of a studio trying to track down Bob Hope to star in a TV special about husband and wife television stars. The first half concerns Lucy's tracking the elusive Hope all around the world. The second half presents the special that they eventually do together—a show based on the Sherwood Schwartz play Mr. and Mrs.

specials.title: Lucy in London
specials.description: Lucy tours mid-'60s London in search of the latest trends, moving through fashion, nightlife, and tourist set pieces with a loose narrative holding it together. The special features British personalities and entertainers of the moment, including appearances by Anthony Newley and Dave Clark, placing Lucy within the city's contemporary pop culture. Expanding on the “trip to London” idea introduced on The Lucy Show, it functions more as a travel showcase than a continuation of the series. Shot on location, it reflects a shift in style and production scale, even if the comedy itself remains familiar.

specials.title: Happy Anniversary and Goodbye
specials.description: Ball steps outside her familiar persona, starring opposite Art Carney as a long-married couple confronting the reality of a relationship that has grown stale. Supporting players like Richard Benjamin help ground the story in a more contemporary tone. The material leans toward light drama with touches of comedy, though it never fully settles into either mode.

specials.title: Lucy Gets Lucky
specials.description: Lucy lands in Las Vegas trying to see Dean Martin perform, with the setting driving a series of escalating mishaps. Martin appears as himself, giving the special a built-in focal point, while the surrounding casino environment provides opportunities for familiar physical comedy. The structure is thin, but the pairing with Martin helps anchor the material.

specials.title: Three for Two
specials.description: This project pairs Ball with Jackie Gleason across a set of relationship-centered stories. Supporting performers vary by segment, but the focus remains on the interaction between Ball and Gleason, whose contrasting styles never fully settle into a consistent rhythm. The appeal lies more in the pairing than in the material itself.

specials.title: What Now, Catherine Curtis?
specials.description: Ball plays a newly divorced woman starting over, opposite Dabney Coleman and Nita Talbot. The supporting cast helps ground the story in a more contemporary setting, though the script occasionally pulls back toward familiar comedic rhythms. It reflects a continued attempt to move beyond the Lucy persona.

specials.title: CBS Salutes Lucy: The First 25 Years
specials.description: Airing in 1976, this CBS tribute marks 25 years of Lucille Ball on the network, spanning I Love Lucy, The Lucy Show, and Here's Lucy. Built around clips, retrospectives, and on-stage segments, the special gathers a wide range of performers and personalities to honor her career, including Milton Berle, Bob Hope, Carol Burnett, Jack Benny, George Burns, Danny Thomas, Dean Martin, Gale Gordon, and Lucie Arnaz, among others. The structure is less about new material than recognition, reinforcing how central Ball had been to CBS's identity for a quarter century. It works best as a curated celebration of her peak years, with the guest appearances underscoring the breadth of her influence across television and comedy.

specials.title: Lucy Calls the President
specials.description: Lucy's complaint escalates into a dinner invitation with the President, with Vivian Vance appearing alongside her in one of their final on-screen collaborations. The supporting cast includes Mary Jane Croft and Kenneth Mars, helping frame the story within a mix of domestic farce and light political satire.

specials.title: Lucy Moves to NBC
specials.description: This special is built around a significant shift in Ball's career: her move to NBC after nearly three decades as a defining presence on CBS. The premise places Lucy inside a television network environment, developing a new show while navigating studio politics and production challenges. Gale Gordon returns as her longtime foil, maintaining a familiar dynamic within a different setting. The concept introduces a meta angle, reflecting Ball's real-world transition, though the execution leans on established comedic rhythms rather than fully exploring the new environment. It stands as a notable late-career pivot, even if the material itself remains rooted in the patterns she had long established.
